title: approvals — compliance evidence
description: This module is a load-bearing compliance-evidence surface. Every SOC 2 CC6.3 access-review control, ISO 27001 A.9.2 user-access-management control, and PCI-DSS 8.5 privileged-access control ties an audit trail requirement to an approval_instance. Retention windows align with SOC 2's 7-year evidence retention on executed approvals and 90-day hold on rejected + expired flows. Subprocessors: NONE — the module runs entirely on Stackra-owned infra + delegates notification transports to the notifications module (which handles subprocessor DPAs at that layer).

controls.evidence: PurgeExpiredApprovalsJob enforces 90-day hold on rejected/expired/cancelled; 7-year hold on executed (evidence requirement); template rows preserved indefinitely (governance audit); approval_decisions retained forever.
The 7-year retention on executed instances is anchored to legitimate-interest for SOC 2 auditor-required retention windows. Tenants using the module inherit the retention contract in their DPA.

Approval contexts inherit the strictest classification tier of any nested field; presenter redacts before wire projection. Approver identities + decision comments are `confidential` tier by default. PCI-DSS scanner refuses instance-create when regulated_financial data leaks into context (fires ApprovalSecurityViolation).

Caller_is_approver invariant enforced by three independent layers (validation rule + middleware + observer). Direct DB writes on approval_decisions refused via observer + fire ApprovalSecurityViolation. request_ip stored as SHA-256 (never plaintext) to prevent leaked-dump correlation.

Every access-mutation action decorated with #[AsApprovableAction] routes through the engine when a template matches. approval_instances table is the canonical logical-access-change ledger; audit reports (POST /platform/approvals/audit-report) surface the full history.

Approval templates encode segregation-of-duties rules via when_expression + selector_expression. E.g. `requester != resource_owner` + `role('finance_manager') and branch_of(requester)` enforces that no one approves their own refund + refunds are decided by an in-branch manager. approval_advanced_expressions entitlement grants access to the full expression set.
All approval-lifecycle events are broadcast over TLS 1.2+ channels. approval_decisions.request_ip_hash never persists plaintext. `payload_json` never wire-projected.

MetricsCollector emits stackra.approvals.* counters (instances by status, decisions by outcome, expression evaluation timings, selector failures). Health probes check queue-depth + reminder-cadence + template-integrity. ReconcileTemplateVersionsJob nightly enforces defensive invariants.

Every access grant (via access/requests → this module) is an approval_instance with SOC 2 evidence: requester_id, resolved_approvers snapshot, decision timeline with comments, final_decider_id, executed_at. Access reviews query the module's audit report.
Impersonation-session extension is an approvable action. Extending beyond default 60m ttl requires a director + compliance approval flow.

The PCI-DSS scanner runs at instance-create time on context_json — any regex match for cardholder-data patterns (PAN, CVV, expiry) fires ApprovalSecurityViolation(violation_type='restricted_context_leak') AND refuses instance creation. Actions decorated with #[AsApprovableAction] are contractually required to strip cardholder data from their approvalContext() output; violations are treated as a P0 security signal.

Privileged actions (identity.impersonation.start, identity.impersonation.extend, access.grants.create with admin permissions) are decorated with #[AsApprovableAction]. Approval flows create the audit trail evidence PCI-DSS auditors require.

controls.principle: audit user access to cardholder data
controls.evidence: Every ApprovalGranted / ApprovalRejected / ApprovalExecuted event is written to the audit log with actor_id + final_decider_id + subject reference. Retention is 7 years (executed) which satisfies PCI-DSS 10.7's 1-year hot requirement + audit-friendly cold storage.

Actions touching minor's data (e.g. sports.athletes.enroll where athlete_age < 13) decorate with #[AsApprovableAction] pointing at templates that require parent-user selector matching. approval_instances.context_json.is_minor=true triggers the platform-level classification uplift to regulated_minor tier.

hipaa.notes: HIPAA support deferred to Wave 3+. When a healthcare-adjacent tenant adopts, PHI-touching actions decorate with #[AsApprovableAction] pointing at templates that require credentialed-provider selectors. approval_instances.context_json inherits regulated_health tier via action's context_variables_schema declaration.

controls.evidence: Data-deletion requests (compliance.data.delete_by_subject) route through the engine when tenant policy requires a Data Protection Officer approval. approval_instances audit-log answers CCPA 1798.130 subject-request-tracking evidence.

executed_instances.notes: 7 years total; hot 2 years for auditor Q1/Q4 sweep + cold 5 years for evidence archive.

rejected_expired_cancelled_instances.notes: SOC 2 evidence typically only needs the completed-flow audit trail; rejections retained for the DPO investigation window.

template_versions.notes: Templates + template_approvers preserved indefinitely; deletion only via cascade on tenant erasure. Old versions retained to reproduce past decisions.

audit_records.notes: owen-it/laravel-auditing audit rows retained 7 years for the executed flow. Rows survive their subject's hard-delete via the audit module's separate retention chain.
